research_question: Within metrical verse specifically, do poems drafted with a line-ending co-writer receive higher blind craft ratings from working poets than poems drafted solo or with a rhyming dictionary?
intervention_primitive: Turn-taking line-ending co-writer: a drafting surface in which the tool proposes three candidate endings per line that scan to a pre-declared meter and rhyme scheme, with the poet accepting, editing, or overriding each proposal, constrained to formalist tradition.
one_sentence_claim: Formalist poems drafted with a meter-aware line-ending co-writer receive higher blind craft ratings from working poets than matched poems drafted solo or with a rhyming dictionary.
why_divergent: Differs from branch A on method_family (expert-rated evaluative comparison vs generative mechanism study) and on intervention_primitive (meter-constrained peer co-writer vs corpus-indexed divergence probe); differs from branch C on human_system_relationship (peer_collaboration vs infrastructural_support) and on research_question (outcome quality vs taxonomy of rejection).

grounding.quoted_claim: Co-writing with an AI writing assistant changed the opinion content of participants' written posts in the direction of the model's configured stance, demonstrating that AI co-writing tools exert measurable influence on textual output.

AI decision-support can erode domain-specific autonomy — comprising skilled competence and authentic value-formation — through mechanisms such as unconscious value shifts, raising design concerns relevant to creative co-writing tools.

Tools past a certain threshold of scale and complexity can reverse from serving human purposes to conditioning humans to serve the tool, a risk applicable to meter-prescriptive co-writing systems that constrain poetic choice.

The authors combine retrospective corpus analysis with a prospective user study to evaluate AI-generated outputs against expert human judgments, providing a methodological template for blind expert rating of AI-assisted creative work.
